source.old_title: Sanitized Chrome for Windows STIG-Style Example
source.new_title: Sanitized Chrome for Windows STIG-Style Example

changes.reason: A new high-severity control was added, so it should be triaged before lower-risk backlog work.
evidence_needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting
GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export

control.title: Chrome extension installation must be restricted
references: https://chromeenterprise.google/policies/ | Chrome Enterprise extension policy reference
control.check_summary: Review Chrome enterprise extension policy and confirm unapproved extension installation is blocked or allowlisted.
control.fix_summary: Configure Chrome ExtensionInstallBlocklist and ExtensionInstallAllowlist policies through GPO or registry.

changes.reason: The control was removed, so downstream tickets, evidence requests, or mappings may need cleanup.
evidence_needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting

control.title: Deprecated Chrome cleanup policy must be reviewed
control.check_summary: Review legacy Chrome cleanup policy settings.
control.fix_summary: Remove or disable legacy Chrome cleanup configuration.

changes.reason: The severity increased to high and the remediation text changed, so old tickets or implementation notes should not be reused without review.
evidence_needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting
GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export

control.title: Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection must be enabled
references: Mitigations: Document any approved exception to the enterprise browser policy baseline.
VulnDiscussion: Browser protections help reduce exposure to known malicious sites, suspicious downloads, and unsafe extensions.
control.check_summary: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel is set to enhanced protection.
control.fix_summary: Configure Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection through Group Policy or the registry policy path and document the applied baseline.

changes.reason: The check procedure changed enough that evidence requests or validation steps may need to be refreshed.
evidence_needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting
GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export

control.title: Chrome password manager must be disabled
control.check_summary: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing PasswordManagerEnabled is disabled for managed users.
control.fix_summary: Disable Chrome Password Manager using enterprise policy.
